w:r.w:t: Coated GCC & PCC Import (Vietnam/Malaysia/Egypt) for Indian Industry · Dual Business
Calcite / Calcium Carbonate — GCC Exported from India · Coated GCC & PCC Imported for Indian Industry
₃ ≥97–99% · GCC uncoated & stearic acid coated · PCC
"Enquire for Supply" — Indian buyers (coated GCC/PCC) and export buyers (India GCC)
"Offer Your Supply" — foreign exporters (Vietnam, Malaysia, Egypt)
teness & particle size tested · MSDS available · Sample in 3–5 days
dual business model — export and import
India both exports calcite/GCC and imports specialty grades — we operate on both sides.
gnificant exporter of ground calcium carbonate (GCC) — 4,880 export shipments in 2023–24, growing at 23% annually. India's Rajasthan and Gujarat deposits produce high-purity calcite (
₃ 97–99%) with good whiteness — competitive for paints, rubber, paper
and construction filler applications in Nepal, Bangladesh, Russia and Africa. Export is completely free — no restrictions, no licensing.
Import side — Specialty coated GCC and PCC:

India imports 10,495 shipments of calcium carbonate annually — primarily coated GCC and PCC from Vietnam, Malaysia and Egypt. Why? Indian domestic deposits lack the whiteness (GE 95+), ultra-fine particle size (D50 1–3 µm) and consistent stearic acid coati

ng quality required by Indian PVC pipe manufacturers, rigid PVC compounders and specialty plastics producers. The PVC and plastics industry specifically demands imported grades because domestic calcite at equivalent particle size and coating quality is not
available at competitive cost. We import and supply these grades to Indian industry.
Section 3 — Understanding the grades: GCC vs PCC vs Coated
Produced by mechanical grinding and air classification of natural calcite or li
mestone — no chemical change. Final product purity and whiteness depends entirely on ore quality. India's Rajasthan calcite produces GCC with
₃ 97–99% and whiteness 88–94 GE — good for paints, rubber and paper at coarser grades. For ultra-fine (D50 bel
µm) with whiteness above 95 GE, Vietnam and Malaysia origin is superior.
Coated GCC — Stearic acid surface treated
When GCC powder is surface-treated with stearic acid or zinc stearate, the hydrophilic surface becomes hydrophobic — this dramatically improves compatibility with polymer matrices (PVC, PP,
). Coated GCC disperses better in plastic compounds, improves

processing, and reduces oil absorption. The PVC pipe industry specifically requires coated GCC — uncoated grade causes processing problems. India imports large volumes of coated GCC from Vietnam and Malaysia.
by chemical synthesis — burning limestone to lime (
reacting with CO₂. The crystal morphology (
ble with GCC. PCC achieves D50 as low as 0.07 µm (

-PCC) with very high whiteness (98+ GE) and low oil absorption after coating. Used in premium PVC, flexible PVC, specialty paper and pharmaceutical applications. India is the world's second-largest PCC
Section 4A — Export grades: GCC from India — full specifications
Export destinations: Nepal (47% of India's exports) · Bangladesh · Russia · Kenya · UAE · Bhutan
, Jodhpur) produces the highest quality domestic GCC — 500+ processors active
le from Rajasthan processors — suitable for rubber and standard plastic applications
All grades: COA per lot with
₃%, whiteness, brightness, D50, D97, moisture, oil absorption
Section 4B — Import grades: Coated GCC & PCC from Vietnam / Malaysia / Egyp
PVC pipe manufacturers: require coated GCC with D50 1–3 µm, whiteness ≥95 GE, stearic acid coating ≥1% — Indian domestic grades cannot consistently match this at competitive price
-PCC (D50 0.05–0.1 µm) for impact modifier effect — not produced domestically at scale
PCC for reinforcement and processing improvement
97+) PCC slurry — Indian GCC too coarse and low brightness for paper coating
, speciality plastics compounders across Gujarat, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu
PVC pipes & fittings (largest Indian buyer segment for imported grades)
in rigid PVC pipes — reduces cost, improves stiffness, and aids processing.
it must be ultra-fine coated GCC or PCC: uncoated or coarse g
rades cause fish-eyes, surface defects and reduced impact strength. Indian pipe manufacturers (Supreme, Astral,
supply chain) import coated GCC from Vietnam and Malaysia specifically because domestic grades do not meet the D50 and coating quality r
GCC used as a functional extender in interior and exterior emulsion paints — improves opacity, reduces
₂ usage, controls sheen and improves scrub resistance. For exterior paints, coarser GCC (D50 1
0–20 µm) is standard; for smooth interior finishes, finer grades (D50 3–8 µm) are used. Indian GCC (Rajasthan origin) is competitive for paint filler applications in Nepal, Bangladesh and Gulf.
GCC as reinforcing filler in rubber — impro

ves tensile strength, hardness and abrasion resistance at lower cost than silica. Coated grades essential for good dispersion. India exports uncoated and coated GCC for rubber applications; also imports finer PCC grades for premium rubber applications.

PCC used as paper filler and coating pigment — improves brightness, smoothness and printability. Ultra-high whiteness (GE 97+) PCC in slurry form is the standard for coated paper and board. India imports PCC for paper applications from Vi

Food-grade and pharma-grade PCC used as calcium supplement, antacid, excipient and stabiliser. Requires strict heavy metals limits and pharmacopoeia compliance (IP/BP/USP). India imports these specialty grades fr
Fixed mine source (Rajasthan/Gujarat) for export grades — consistent
Fixed origin (Vietnam/Malaysia) for imported grades — consistent particle size lot to lot
₃%, whiteness, brightness, D50, D97, moisture, oil absorption
SGS / Intertek inspection available on request
MSDS and packing list with every consignment
Both export (India GCC) and import (coated/PCC) grades from one supplier

India is one of the world's largest importers of calcium carbonate — 10,495 shipments annually and growing at 34%. Vietnam, Malaysia and Egypt currently dominate supply, and with India's PVC, plastics and paints industries expanding rapidly, this demand is only going to grow.
If you produce coated GCC, PCC or specialty calcium carbonate grades and are exploring the Indian market,
we are a buyer worth knowing.
We are looking for suppliers who can work with us on credit-based payment terms (DA / Net 45- 60 days) — if this matches how you work with established buyers, we would be glad to hear from you.
What we look for in a supplier:
₃ ≥98% · Whiteness ≥95 GE · D50 1–5 µm · Stearic acid coating ≥1% · COA per lot
₃ ≥98% · Whiteness ≥96 GE · D50 0.5–2 µm · COA per lot
Company · Country · Product (coated GCC / PCC / uncoated GCC) · D50 · Whiteness GE · Monthly capacity (MT) · FOB price · Payment terms · Contac
ection 8 — Mine owners & processors (for India GCC export)
) and Gujarat processors for export to Nepal, Bangladesh and other markets. If you have a
unit with consistent quality and monthly output, we want to hear from you.
≥97% · Whiteness ≥88 GE · Consistent mesh size · Monthly capacity ≥50 MT · Contact number
Transit: Bangladesh 5–8 days · Nepal road · Russia 20–28 days
HS Code: 2836.50 (calcium carbonate) · 2517.20 (marble granules/powder) depending on grade
Import (Coated GCC / PCC from Vietnam/Malaysia for Indian
Packaging: 25kg bags in 1MT jumbo bags (standard for PCC) · 50kg bags for coated GCC
Lead time: ex-stock 3–7 days · fresh import
Why does India import calcium carbonate?
are imported for PVC and premium applications.
Q2. What is the difference between GCC and PCC?
→ synthetic, ultra-fine, better performance but higher cost
Q3. Why is coated GCC required for PVC?
Uncoated GCC can cause defects and
Standard paints → whiteness 88–92 GE, D50 10–20 µm
Premium paints → higher whiteness and finer particle size
